validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile on both new files exits 0 (0.15s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_teardown_batch passes 10/10 (4.4s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 144/144 up from 134 (27.8s)

checkpoint.summary: unit_021 complete. New skills/development-harness/scripts/teardown_batch.py (146 LOC, stdlib-only) provides idempotent cleanup of harness batch worktrees and branches with scoped (batch_id='<id>') and global (batch_id=None) modes. Every git invocation runs with check=False so already-gone state never raises. Orphaned on-disk worktree dirs are scrubbed via shutil.rmtree after best-effort `git worktree remove --force`; orphaned branches (no worktree) are still deleted by the second pass. Final `git worktree prune` resyncs git metadata. 10 new tests across 4 classes verify idempotence (second run is empty), scope isolation, missing-state tolerance, and CLI end-to-end. Test suite 134 -> 144.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_022 (PHASE_005): scope-violation detector in merge_batch.py. Before each merge, run `git diff --name-only <merge-base>..<branch>` and reject any unit whose diff includes files matching none of the unit's declared touches_paths globs. The sub-agent self-report is never trusted for blast radius. Rejected units are marked failed with category='scope_violation'.
